body.question_1: How would you rate the university's communication with parents regarding your child's academic progress and welfare?
body.answer_1: I am insufficiently concerned about the lack of meaningful communication from the School of Information Technology regarding my child's academic progress and overall welfare. The parent portal is difficult to navigate, frequently displays outdated information, and does not provide the real-time updates that are essential for engaged parental monitoring. I have sent multiple queries to the Academic Affairs Office over the past term and received responses that were delayed by weeks or were entirely generic. As a parent who has invested significantly in my child's education, I expect timely, accurate, and personalized communication that reflects a genuine partnership between the university and the families it serves. Urgent improvements to both the portal and communication processes are needed.

body.question_2: How satisfied are you with the quality of education your child is receiving at this university?
body.answer_2: The teaching quality in my Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning courses at the School of Information Technology has been remarkable this semester. My lecturers consistently demonstrate deep subject matter expertise and present complex material in a clear, well-structured, and engaging manner that truly enhances comprehension. I particularly praise the integration of real-world industry examples and hands-on exercises into every session, which makes abstract theoretical concepts genuinely applicable. The interactive teaching style — characterized by open discussions, collaborative problem-solving, and prompt feedback on assignments — has significantly accelerated my academic growth. The faculty's visible dedication and responsiveness to students makes me confident I am receiving a high-calibre education that will serve me well in my future career.

body.question_3: How confident are you in the university's ability to prepare your child for future career opportunities?
body.answer_3: Career preparation support from the Career Center at the School of Information Technology has been inadequate and has left many students — including myself — feeling ill-equipped for the competitive graduate employment market. Career workshops are infrequent, poorly promoted, and often scheduled at times that conflict with lecture commitments, reducing student participation. The internship database is outdated and contains many positions that are no longer active, and support for students during internship placements is minimal to nonexistent. I raise concerns about the university to substantially reinvest in the Career Center — expanding its employer network, modernizing its tools, and increasing the frequency and quality of career development programming across all academic programs and year levels.

body.question_4: How would you assess the campus safety measures and student welfare programs in place at this institution?
body.answer_4: Campus safety and student welfare at the School of Information Technology are remarkable, and I feel confident that my child is studying in a secure and well-monitored environment that prioritizes student well-being. Security personnel are visibly present across campus during all hours, CCTV coverage is comprehensive, and well-lit pathways ensure safe movement even during evening hours. The Student Affairs Office runs an impressive range of student welfare programs including health screenings, mental health workshops, peer support networks, and emergency assistance protocols that demonstrate a genuine institutional commitment to holistic student care. Knowing that these resources exist and are actively promoted gives me great peace of mind as a parent.

body.question_5: How transparent and straightforward have you found the university's financial policies, fee management, and scholarship communication?
body.answer_5: Financial management by the Finance Department at the School of Information Technology has been acceptable from my perspective as a parent, meeting the fundamental requirements of tuition billing and payment processing without notable excellence or failure. Fee statements are generally accurate, though occasional discrepancies have required follow-up that is more time-consuming than it should be. Communication about scholarship timelines could be significantly improved — families benefit greatly from advance notice when disbursement dates shift or eligibility criteria change, and this is currently lacking. I would welcome a dedicated parent-facing financial FAQ and communication channel to reduce the need for direct contact for routine financial queries.

body.question_6: How effective do you find the parent portal and digital communication systems provided by the university?
body.answer_6: The parent portal at the School of Information Technology functions at a adequate level that provides access to essential information but lacks the polish and reliability expected of a modern institutional digital platform. Grade and attendance data are generally accessible, though refresh rates can be slow and the interface requires familiarity before becoming navigable. The portal is not optimized for mobile devices, which is a significant limitation given that most parents primarily use smartphones for digital access. Push notification functionality exists but is inconsistently triggered, meaning important updates are sometimes missed. A targeted UX improvement initiative — focusing on mobile responsiveness, navigation clarity, and reliable notifications — would greatly increase parental engagement and satisfaction with the digital experience.

body.question_7: How satisfied are you with the academic counseling and advising support your child receives?
body.answer_7: Academic counseling and advising services at the School of Information Technology, coordinated by the Academic Affairs Office, have been commendable in supporting my child's academic planning and decision-making throughout the year. Assigned academic advisors are knowledgeable, approachable, and proactively schedule meetings at key decision points in the academic calendar — such as course selection, internship planning, and graduation preparation. The quality and consistency of advice my child has received has given our family confidence that their academic trajectory is being thoughtfully guided by experienced professionals who understand both the program requirements and the student's individual goals. This service is clearly a strength of the institution.

body.question_8: How well does the university address students' mental health and personal well-being needs?
body.answer_8: Student support services at the School of Information Technology are fair in their scope and delivery, covering the essential categories but lacking depth and proactivity in several important areas that affect student well-being. Counseling services exist and are functional, but appointment availability is limited and wait times are often longer than appropriate for students in need. Financial support processes are reasonably accessible but require significant paperwork and involve delays that reduce their effectiveness in urgent situations. Investing in additional counseling staff, simplifying support request processes, and proactively promoting available services — especially during high-stress periods like mid-terms — would markedly improve the student experience.

body.question_9: How would you evaluate the quality and suitability of the university's physical facilities and campus environment?
body.answer_9: Campus facilities at the School of Information Technology, maintained by the Facilities Management, are generally moderate and adequate for day-to-day academic activities, though several areas require attention and investment to meet growing needs. Core academic spaces — including lecture halls and laboratories — are functional and reasonably maintained, though some equipment is beginning to show age and inconsistency in performance. Student study and relaxation spaces are insufficient during peak periods, and the condition of older campus buildings varies considerably. A systematic facilities audit followed by a prioritized improvement plan would help address longstanding issues and ensure the campus environment supports the academic ambitions of all students more effectively.

body.question_10: What improvements would you suggest to enhance the university's support for both students and their families?
body.answer_10: My overall assessment of the School of Information Technology as a parent is that it offers a adequate educational experience with genuine strengths that are unfortunately balanced by structural weaknesses that need sustained attention. The academic programs are broadly well-designed, and there are individual faculty members and support staff who clearly go above and beyond for students. However, system-level inconsistencies in service quality, communication, and resource provision create an uneven experience that depends too heavily on which individual staff members a student encounters. I encourage the institution to invest in building reliable systems and consistent standards that ensure every student — regardless of program or year — receives the same high-quality support and educational experience.
